sections.content:
The board didn't change (board_changed: false). So RIGHT didn't move either square. The left square is at row=41, col=21 and the right square is at row=46, col=36. Neither moved. So RIGHT doesn't work for either square at their current positions.
Let me re-examine the maze structure more carefully. Looking at the image:
- The left blue square is in a yellow corridor that goes UP and RIGHT didn't move it. Let me try DOWN and LEFT to see what directions are open.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The squares move 5 cells per action. The left square is at row=41, col=21. Looking at the maze, it seems to be in a corridor that goes up. But UP didn't move it. Let me try DOWN to try DOWN.
Wait, let me re-examine. The left square was at row=41 after the first UP action (from row=46). Then 3 more UPs didn't move it. Then RIGHT didn't move it. So the square seems to be at row=41, col=21. Let me try DOWN to see if it can go back down.
Actually, let me re-examine the maze structure. The left square is in a yellow area. The yellow area has a corridor that goes up from the bottom, then curves right, then up to the top. The square is currently in the vertical section. But UP didn't move it further up is blocked. So it needs to go RIGHT to enter the horizontal corridor, then UP.
But RIGHT didn't work. Let me check if the square needs to be at a different position to turn right. Let me try DOWN to see if it can move down, then RIGHT from there.
Actually, wait - looking more carefully at the image, the left blue square is in a yellow region that's surrounded by black on the left and right. The yellow region goes up and curves right. The square is at a position where it can go up or down. But UP didn't move it (the square can only move in the direction of the corridor. At row=41, col=21, the corridor goes up. But the square should be able to go up. But it didn't. Let me check if the square is already at the top of its current corridor section.
Let me try DOWN to see if the square can move down.
